srcEid: The source chain endpoint ID.  - sender: The sender address on the src chain.  - nonce: The nonce of the message._message The lzReceive payload.Applications can optionally choose to implement separate composeMsg senders that are NOT the bridging layer.The default sender IS the OAppReceiver implementer.","params":{"_sender":"The sender address."},"returns":{"_0":"isSender Is a valid sender."}},"lzReceive((uint32,bytes32,uint64),bytes32,bytes,address,bytes)":{"details":"Entry point for receiving messages or packets from the endpoint.Entry point for receiving msg/packet from the LayerZero endpoint.","params":{"_executor":"The address of the executor for the received message.","_extraData":"Additional arbitrary data provided by the corresponding executor.","_guid":"The unique identifier for the received LayerZero message.","_message":"The payload of the received message.","_origin":"The origin information containing the source endpoint and sender address.  - srcEid: The source chain endpoint ID.  - sender: The sender address on the src chain.  - nonce: The nonce of the message."}},"nextNonce(uint32,bytes32)":{"details":"_srcEid The source endpoint ID._sender The sender address.The path nonce starts from 1. If 0 is returned it means that there is NO nonce ordered enforcement.Is required by the off-chain executor to determine the OApp expects msg execution is ordered.This is also enforced by the OApp.By default this is NOT enabled. ie. nextNonce is hardcoded to return 0.","returns":{"nonce":"The next nonce."}},"oAppVersion()":{"returns":{"receiverVersion":"The version of the OAppReceiver.sol implementation.","senderVersion":"The version of the OAppSender.sol implementation."}},"owner()":{"details":"Returns the address of the current owner."},"quoteCrossChainClaim((uint32,bytes,(uint256,address,(uint256,address,uint8),bytes32[],bool)[])[])":{"params":{"params":"The claim parameters grouped per destination."},"returns":{"nativeFee":"The native token fee required."}},"renounceOwnership()":{"details":"Leaves the contract without owner. It will not be possible to call `onlyOwner` functions anymore. Can only be called by the current owner. NOTE: Renouncing ownership will leave the contract without an owner, thereby removing any functionality that is only available to the owner."},"setDelegate(address)":{"details":"Only the owner/admin of the OApp can call this function.Provides the ability for a delegate to set configs, on behalf of the OApp, directly on the Endpoint contract.","params":{"_delegate":"The address of the delegate to be set."}},"setPeer(uint32,bytes32)":{"details":"Only the owner/admin of the OApp can call this function.Indicates that the peer is trusted to send LayerZero messages to this OApp.Set this to bytes32(0) to remove the peer address.Peer is a bytes32 to accommodate non-evm chains.","params":{"_eid":"The endpoint ID.","_peer":"The address of the peer to be associated with the corresponding endpoint."}},"transferOwnership(address)":{"details":"Transfers ownership of the contract to a new account (`newOwner`). allowInitializePath((uint32,bytes32,uint.details: This indicates to the endpoint that the OApp has enabled msgs for this particular path to be received.This defaults to assuming if a peer has been set, its initialized. Can be overridden by the OApp if there is other logic to determine this.

params.origin: The origin information containing the source endpoint and sender address.

returns._0: Whether the path has been initialized.

batchCrossChainClaimPayout((uint32,bytes.details: All claims must be from the same caller (designated recipient).

params.params: Array of claim parameters grouped per destination.

params._endpoint: The address of the LayerZero endpoint on this chain.
params._factory: The address of the AmpleEarnFactory contract on this chain.
params._owner: The owner of the contract (can configure OApp peers).

isComposeMsgSender((uint32,bytes32,uint6.details: _origin The origin information containing the source endpoint and sender address.  - srcEid: The source chain endpoint ID.  - sender: The sender address on the src chain.  - nonce: The nonce of the message._message The lzReceive payload.Applications can optionally choose to implement separate composeMsg senders that are NOT the bridging layer.The default sender IS the OAppReceiver implementer.

lzReceive((uint32,bytes32,uint64),bytes3.details: Entry point for receiving messages or packets from the endpoint.Entry point for receiving msg/packet from the LayerZero endpoint.

params._executor: The address of the executor for the received message.
params._extraData: Additional arbitrary data provided by the corresponding executor.
params._guid: The unique identifier for the received LayerZero message.
params._message: The payload of the received message.
params._origin: The origin information containing the source endpoint and sender address.  - srcEid: The source chain endpoint ID.  - sender: The sender address on the src chain.  - nonce: The nonce of the message.

nextNonce(uint32,bytes32).details: _srcEid The source endpoint ID._sender The sender address.The path nonce starts from 1. If 0 is returned it means that there is NO nonce ordered enforcement.Is required by the off-chain executor to determine the OApp expects msg execution is ordered.This is also enforced by the OApp.By default this is NOT enabled. ie. nextNonce is hardcoded to return 0.

returns.receiverVersion: The version of the OAppReceiver.sol implementation.
returns.senderVersion: The version of the OAppSender.sol implementation.

owner().details: Returns the address of the current owner.

params.params: The claim parameters grouped per destination.

renounceOwnership().details: Leaves the contract without owner. It will not be possible to call `onlyOwner` functions anymore. Can only be called by the current owner. NOTE: Renouncing ownership will leave the contract without an owner, thereby removing any functionality that is only available to the owner.

setDelegate(address).details: Only the owner/admin of the OApp can call this function.Provides the ability for a delegate to set configs, on behalf of the OApp, directly on the Endpoint contract.

params._delegate: The address of the delegate to be set.

setPeer(uint32,bytes32).details: Only the owner/admin of the OApp can call this function.Indicates that the peer is trusted to send LayerZero messages to this OApp.Set this to bytes32(0) to remove the peer address.Peer is a bytes32 to accommodate non-evm chains.

params._peer: The address of the peer to be associated with the corresponding endpoint.

transferOwnership(address).details: Transfers ownership of the contract to a new account (`newOwner`). Can only be called by the current owner.

allowInitializePath((uint32,bytes32,uint.notice: Checks if the path initialization is allowed based on the provided origin.

batchCrossChainClaimPayout((uint32,bytes.notice: Batch initiate cross-chain payout claims to multiple chains.

endpoint().notice: Retrieves the LayerZero endpoint associated with the OApp.

factory().notice: The AmpleEarnFactory contract address on this chain.

isComposeMsgSender((uint32,bytes32,uint6.notice: Indicates whether an address is an approved composeMsg sender to the Endpoint.

localEid().notice: The local chain's LayerZero endpoint ID.

nextNonce(uint32,bytes32).notice: Retrieves the next nonce for a given source endpoint and sender address.

peers(uint32).notice: Retrieves the peer (OApp) associated with a corresponding endpoint.

quoteCrossChainClaim((uint32,bytes,(uint.notice: Quote the fee required to send a cross-chain claim.

setDelegate(address).notice: Sets the delegate address for the OApp.

setPeer(uint32,bytes32).notice: Sets the peer address (OApp instance) for a corresponding endpoint.
